content.text:
Memory stage: NoteReconciler v1.
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Decide how candidate notes should update active note memory.

        Return JSON:
        {
          "operations": [
            {
              "action": "insert | noop | supersede | retract | update",
              "candidate_index": 0,
              "target_note_id": "existing-note-id-or-null",
              "patch": null,
              "links_to_create": [
                {
                  "to_note_id": "existing-note-id",
                  "link_type": "supports | contradicts | refines | related | supersedes | derived_from",
                  "link_weight": 1.0
                }
              ],
              "reason": "short explanation"
            }
          ]
        }

        Actions:
        - insert: candidate is new reusable context.
        - noop: candidate duplicates an existing active note or is lower value.
        - supersede: candidate is a better current version of target_note_id.
        - update: patch target_note_id without inserting a new note.
        - retract: target_note_id should no longer be active because TARGET_SOURCE retracts it.

        Rules:
        - Compare semantic meaning, not wording.
        - Do not keep duplicate notes just because titles differ.
        - Prefer updating or superseding an existing note when the candidate refines the same decision, rationale, plan, or procedure.
        - Insert when the candidate captures a distinct reusable semantic fragment.
        - Use noop for summary spam, weak paraphrases, or information already captured by an existing note.
        - Use links_to_create only for strong semantic relations between notes.
        - target_note_id must be one of Existing active notes below or null.
        - candidate_index is zero-based from Candidate notes below.
        - Return one operation per candidate unless TARGET_SOURCE only retracts or updates an existing note.
        - Return valid JSON only.

        TARGET_SOURCE:
        Document title: 01. Architecture
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
Document section: 01. Architecture / 15. Retention / forgetting .. 16. Optional fast/slow execution path
Document lines: 509-546

## 15. Retention / forgetting

В `v3` forgetting — не случайное “пусть старое реже ищется”, а policy.

Минимальные действия:
- downgrade retrieval state
- archive
- soft delete
- hard delete by request

Минимальные триггеры:
- namespace policy
- age
- low use_count
- legal/privacy request
- explicit user forget/delete command

## 16. Optional fast/slow execution path

Это уже не обязательный MVP, но хороший следующий шаг.

### Fast path
- user-facing
- lower latency
- cheap retrieval
- cheap draft
- escalation gate

### Slow path
- stronger model / worker
- conflict resolution
- answer verification
- note consolidation
- memory repair

### Важный момент
Между fast и slow path лучше гонять **typed handoff objects**, а не свободный текст.

        Candidate notes:
        [0] type=DIRECTION title=v3 forgetting is an explicit retention policy with defined actions and triggers summary=In v3 memory architecture, forgetting is treated as an explicit policy (not passive decay) with minimum actions (downgrade retrieval state, archive, soft delete, hard delete by request) and minimum triggers (namespace policy, age, low use_count, legal/privacy requests, explicit user forget/delete command). scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 15. Retention / forgetting .. 16. Optional fast/slow execution path' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md entities=entity:<0>,entity:<1> keywords=v3 forgetting policy,retention triggers,delete actions tags=memory_policy,retention,forgetting,v3 evidence=В `v3` forgetting — не случайное “пусть старое реже ищется”, а policy.
[1] type=DIRECTION title=Optional fast/slow execution split with typed handoff between paths summary=The architecture proposes an optional next-step split into Fast path (user-facing, low-latency, cheap retrieval/draft, escalation gate) and Slow path (stronger worker/model, conflict resolution, answer verification, note consolidation, memory repair), with a key rule to pass typed handoff objects instead of free text between paths. scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 15. Retention / forgetting .. 16. Optional fast/slow execution path' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md entities=entity:<2>,entity:<3>,entity:<4> keywords=fast path,slow path,typed handoff objects tags=execution_model,latency,verification,handoff evidence=Между fast и slow path лучше гонять **typed handoff objects**, а не свободный текст.

        Existing active notes:
        id=hot-path:note:<0> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Responsibility split across runtime, memory worker, and storage layer summary=`User-facing runtime` handles dialogue, tool orchestration, read-time retrieval, and cheap routing/escalation, and must avoid heavy reconciliation/repair. `Memory worker / manager` owns routing, retrieval-before-update, canonicalization, extraction, reconciliation, consolidation, repair, and retention. `Storage layer` is responsible for immutable evidence, typed tables, transactional updates, line...[truncated 30 chars] scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 10. Разделение ответственности' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=User-facing runtime,Memory worker / manager,Storage layer,separation of responsibilities tags=architecture,component-roles,operating-model useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<1> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Recommended architecture pipeline and component roles summary=The recommended flow routes source inputs through Memory Router and Write-Time Retrieval into Entity Canonicalizer, then branches to Claim/Task/Profile/Note processing with reconciliers writing to Postgres + pgvector; background workers perform consolidation, repair, and retention, while runtime answering uses Read-Time Retrieval Planner and Answer Composer. scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 3. Основная модель записи .. 4. Рекомендуемая архитектура' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=Memory Router,Entity Canonicalizer,Postgres + pgvector,Read-Time Retrieval Planner tags=architecture_diagram,pipeline,component_roles useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<2> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Read path uses mode selection plus v3 budget caps and cautious answer composition summary=The read path requires deciding whether memory is needed, loading core blocks, selecting retrieval mode (Claim-first, Note-first, Mixed, Evidence-first/fallback), enforcing explicit v3 per-mode retrieval caps, and composing answers that track verification status, conflicts, scope, abstention, and note-vs-fact distinction. scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 11. Write path .. 12. Read path' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=read path,retrieval mode,budget caps,answer composer,v3 tags=architecture,retrieval_policy,answering useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<3> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Criteria for choosing Claim vs Note vs both summary=The architecture defines a write policy: use Claim for explicit, normalizable, precise, comparable information; use Note for important retrieval-useful context that is too rich or too early for hard facts; and write both when material contains both explicit facts/tasks and rationale/direction. scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 7. Когда писать `Claim`, а когда `Note`' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=Claim vs Note,write policy,rationale,hard fact tags=memory_architecture,write_path,policy useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<4> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Note maturity lifecycle prevents summary-store drift summary=The architecture defines a Note lifecycle (`fresh` → `stabilizing` → `mature` → `consolidated`) to keep notes as high-signal memory artifacts rather than a generic summary store. Maturity should increase with repeated retrieval use, multi-source support, non-conflict with new data, time without supersede, and recurring rationale value; it should decrease or close on explicit supersede, stale scope...[truncated 30 chars] scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 13. Note maturity .. 14. Memory repair' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=Note maturity,fresh,stabilizing,mature,consolidated,evidence support tags=memory_architecture,note_lifecycle,retrieval_quality useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<5> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=STABILIZING title=Write path is an 8-step transactional pipeline with retrieval-before-extraction summary=The write path is a strict 8-step flow: persist source, route (`noop`/`direct_structured_write`/`note_write`/`mixed`), retrieve candidate memories before extraction, canonicalize entities, extract typed candidates, reconcile with existing items, apply all mutations in one transaction, then schedule maintenance jobs. scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 11. Write path .. 12. Read path' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=append-only,reconciliation,retrieval-before-update,mutation policy,summary spam tags=architecture,memory-write-policy,reconciliation useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<6> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Five-tier memory model separates prompt context, typed memory, notes, evidence, and indexes summary=The architecture defines five storage tiers: Tier A for minimal in-context prompt blocks, Tier B for typed long-term records (`Entity`, `Claim`, `Profile`, `Task`, `Episode`, `Source`, `MemoryRun`), Tier C for semantic note structures (`Note`, `NoteSource`, `NoteEntity`, `NoteLink`), Tier D for immutable raw evidence, and Tier E for retrieval indexes over stored data. scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 5. Storage tiers .. 6. Structured scope' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=Storage tiers,Tier A,Tier B,Tier C,Tier D,Tier E tags=architecture,memory_model,storage_tiers useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<7> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=v3 requires explicit dual scope representation with minimum scope_json fields summary=Compared with `v2`, `v3` makes scope explicit by requiring both `scope_text` (human boundary explanation) and `scope_json` (machine envelope) with at least `scope_kind`, `project_id`, `environment`, `subject_entity_id`, `conversation_id`, `modality`, and `audience` to improve reconciliation and reduce retrieval overgeneralization or false note conflicts. scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 5. Storage tiers .. 6. Structured scope' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=v3,structured scope,scope_json,scope_text,reconciliation,retrieval tags=scope,architecture,design_direction useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<8> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Memory repair is a separate workflow from consolidation summary=`Memory repair` is defined as a distinct background workflow focused on memory health issues (duplicates, contradictions, profile drift, stale tasks, bad entity merges, note spam, and retrieval budget leakage). The document separates it from consolidation: consolidation enriches memory, while repair restores/maintains correctness and hygiene. scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 13. Note maturity .. 14. Memory repair' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=Memory repair,background workflow,duplicate notes,contradictory active items,budget leakage tags=memory_architecture,repair_workflow,data_hygiene useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<9> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Two semantic write paths and two maintenance paths define memory flow summary=The architecture explicitly separates memory handling into two semantic write paths (Direct structured path and Note path) and two maintenance paths (Consolidation and Repair), establishing when to store hard structured data versus contextual notes and how quality is maintained over time. scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 3. Основная модель записи .. 4. Рекомендуемая архитектура' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=Direct structured path,Note path,Consolidation,Repair tags=memory_architecture,write_paths,maintenance_paths useCount=0

responseFormat.description: Reconcile note candidates against active memory notes.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_reconciler

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

structured.fullText: {"operations":[{"action":"insert","candidate_index":0,"links_to_create":[{"link_type":"related","link_weight":0.71,"to_note_id":"hot-path:note:<4>"},{"link_type":"related","link_weight":0.63,"to_note_id":"hot-path:note:<8>"}],"patch":null,"reason":"New distinct policy detail: explicit v3 forgetting actions/triggers is not captured in existing active notes.","target_note_id":null},{"action":"insert","candidate_index":1,"links_to_create":[{"link_type":"refines","link_weight":0.81,"to_note_id":"hot-path:note:<0>"},{"link_type":"related","link_weight":0.58,"to_note_id":"hot-path:note:<8>"}],"patch":null,"reason":"Adds new optional execution-mode split (fast/slow) plus typed handoff rule, which is not explicitly represented yet.","target_note_id":null}]}
